note: Exploration authored by lead from a thorough in-session investigation of the announcement pattern; no redundant code-explorer fan-out was needed because the full mechanism, precedent, send/render sites, i18n, and test conventions were already mapped and are recorded in spec.md ## Technical Approach.

similar_features.canonical_precedent: group_renamed structured announcement — end-to-end display-only kind-9 timeline notice attributed to the protocol sender

files.parse: app/src/lib/marmot/parseStructured.ts (StructuredContent union L16-24, guards L26-55, resolveCancellerDisplay L8-14)
files.i18n: app/src/lib/i18n.ts (Copy type renamedGroupAnnouncement L216, en L878, de L1441)
files.send_site: app/pages/groups.tsx handleRename L452-496 (send L468-470); handleApproveRequest L391-399; sendAnnouncementRef wiring L280 + ChatSendMessageCapture L842
files.approval_impl: app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx approveJoinRequestImpl L495-577 (seeds provisional MemberProfile from request.nickname)

architecture.paradigm: React + context providers; MLS group messaging via marmot-ts; kind-9 application rumors as the chat substrate
architecture.announcement_flow: actor sends structured kind-9 via ChatStore.sendMessage (optimistic append + persist for actor) -> remote members ingest via applicationRumorDispatcher -> ChatBox.renderStructuredMessage branches on parseStructured(content) and renders a presentational component; actor attribution always from msg.senderPubkey (protocol-enforced)

testing_and_conventions.unit: vitest, tests/unit/**/*.test.ts; hooks tested via pure-function extraction (no jsdom/@testing-library). parseStructured is a pure function — direct unit test. Structured-message parse tests already exist for siblings.
testing_and_conventions.e2e: Playwright under app/tests/e2e/. Bucket split by FILENAME: groups-*.spec.ts runs in the Docker relay bucket. Publish-through-the-app rule (no raw WebSocket). Full gate = make test-e2e-all (60 tests).
testing_and_conventions.i18n_rule: All user-facing strings via i18n.ts en+de; never hardcode in components.

prior_learnings.relevance: inviteByNpub (called by approveJoinRequest) commits the Add and memberPubkeys immediately at approval time, not on invitee accept — so the approval moment is a valid, well-defined point to fire the announcement.
member display-name resolution keys on the signed author pubkey; the announcement's structured.pubkey is the admitted member's hex, resolved against profileMap the same way siblings do.
e2e: do NOT close the requester's BrowserContext before/around approval — it destroys MLS KeyPackage material and masks bugs. Keep contexts open while asserting the announcement renders.
e2e propagation: poll live-updating UI with retrying toBeVisible; avoid fixed waits and reload-based polling.
